Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and low urine specific gravity, indicating significant renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) and elevated urinary bilirubin suggest possible underlying renal and/or hepatic dysfunction. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and further assessment of liver function and anemia status.
